$comment: Normatively defined by the AIP Credential Token rules. Credential Tokens are JWTs — signing uses standard JWT compact serialisation, not custom canonicalization. Header requirements, catalog-derived TTL rules, the Credential Token Validation Algorithm, and DPoP validation rules are defined in the draft text.
title: AIP Credential Token Payload
description: The JWT payload of an AIP Credential Token. The full Credential Token is a compact JWT: base64url(header).base64url(payload).signature. JWT Header MUST contain typ='AIP+JWT', alg='EdDSA', and kid as a DID URL for the signing agent key, for example did:aip:personal:9f3a1c82b4e6d7f0a2b5c8e1d4f7a0b3#key-1. Step Execution Tokens are Registry-issued JWTs with a separate schema and validation profile. This schema validates the decoded Credential Token payload only.

aip_version.description: AIP protocol compatibility version. MUST be "0.3" for this spec. This is not the Internet-Draft revision suffix.
aip_version.$comment: Increment enum when a new protocol compatibility version is published, not for every Internet-Draft revision.

iss.description: AID of the agent issuing and signing this token. MUST match the AID identified by the kid header field. MUST equal the token sub claim and the sub field of the last element of aip_chain (verified by Validation Algorithm Step 5g and Step 8 Post-Checks A and B).

sub.description: AID of the acting leaf agent this token represents. MUST equal iss for both direct and delegated agent-issued Credential Tokens. This specification does not define an on-behalf-of subject distinct from the signing agent.

oneOf.description: Single audience — the relying party's identifier.
Multiple audiences. MUST include the relying party's identifier.

aud.description: Intended audience. The relying party MUST reject any token where its own identifier is not present in this field (Validation Algorithm Step 5d). Tokens with a mismatched aud return invalid_token.

iat.description: Issued-at time as a Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch). MUST NOT be in the future — tokens with future iat are rejected (Step 5a, clock skew tolerance: 30 seconds).

exp.description: Expiry time as a Unix timestamp. MUST be strictly greater than iat (zero-duration tokens rejected, Step 2a and Step 5b). TTL limits are derived from ttl_max_seconds in the synced AIP Scope Catalog. When multiple scopes are present, the most restrictive TTL applies. Checked in Step 2a/Step 5c (token_expired) and Step 6 (TTL cap).

jti.description: JWT ID — UUID v4 in canonical lowercase hyphenated format. Used for replay prevention. Relying parties MUST maintain a replay cache keyed by (iss, jti) pairs for the token's validity window. Tokens whose (iss, jti) pair has been seen before return token_replayed. Keying by issuer prevents false rejections from UUID collisions across different agents. Issuers MUST generate jti values that are globally unique within the issuer AID across all delegation chains, principals, grants, scope sets, and audiences.

items.description: A scope string in dot-notation format (e.g., email.read, transactions, web.browse, transactions.v2). Each segment begins with a lowercase letter or underscore and may then contain lowercase letters, digits, or underscores. Concrete registered values and metadata come from the synced AIP Scope Catalog.

aip_scope.description: Capability scopes requested for this interaction. MUST be a subset of the agent's Capability Manifest (verified in Steps 9 and 9a using the scope-to-manifest mapping). Each scope MUST match a defined identifier from the synced AIP Scope Catalog or an explicitly documented local extension. Duplicate scope strings are prohibited. Returns insufficient_scope on mismatch.

items.description: A compact-serialised signed JWT (header.payload.signature) encoding one AIP Principal Token conforming to principal-token.schema.json. Each element MUST be a valid JWT whose signature is verifiable using the public key of the issuer: the root principal's DID-resolved public key for index 0, or the AIP Registry-resolved historical public key of the parent agent AID for delegated elements. Elements MUST be ordered from root (index 0, delegation_depth=0) to the acting leaf agent (index n-1).

aip_chain.description: Ordered array of compact-serialised Principal Token JWTs forming the delegation chain. Minimum: 1 element (direct principal delegation). Maximum: 11 elements (corresponding to the hard maximum delegation depth of 10 — depth-10 agent has 11 chain elements including the root). Validated by Validation Algorithm Step 8. Chains exceeding 11 elements return delegation_chain_invalid.

aip_originator_aid.description: OPTIONAL generally; REQUIRED when the agent is acting in a delegated agent-to-agent workflow. Originating agent AID when the signing agent is acting in a delegated A2A workflow. When present, the value MUST NOT equal the Credential Token sub claim, MUST identify the originator whose Principal Token is carried as originator context in aip_chain, and Relying Parties MAY use it for originator-level policy and audit attribution. Full chain validation remains required.

aip_registry.description: OPTIONAL. Advisory URI of the AIP Registry where this agent's public key and revocation status can be verified. When present, Validation Algorithm Step 6a is REQUIRED and the value MUST match the principal DID Document's AIPRegistry service endpoint before the Relying Party treats it as authoritative. Relying parties MAY choose to verify against a different Registry.

aip_principal_assertion.description: OPTIONAL for Tier 1 and Tier 2; REQUIRED for Tier 3. Compact-serialised signed JWT issued by an enterprise IdP, either an OIDC ID Token or JWT-formatted assertion. The issuer MUST NOT be the AIP Registry. Relying Parties verify the assertion signature against the IdP JWKS and require assertion sub to match the root Principal Token principal.id in aip_chain[0].

aip_engagement_id.description: OPTIONAL. The engagement_id of the Engagement Object this token is scoped to. When present, the Relying Party MUST verify the Engagement is active via GET /v1/engagements/{aip_engagement_id} and must reject with engagement_terminated, engagement_suspended, or engagement_participant_removed as applicable.

jwt_header_requirements.$comment: JWT header requirements for the AIP Credential Token (not validated by this payload schema — header is validated by Validation Algorithm Steps 1-2): typ MUST be 'AIP+JWT'; alg MUST be 'EdDSA'; kid MUST be a DID URL in format did:aip:<namespace>:<32hex>#key-<n>.

ttl_rules.$comment: TTL enforcement: max exp-iat is the minimum ttl_max_seconds value across all requested scopes in the synced AIP Scope Catalog. When mixed, most restrictive applies.

dpop_requirement.$comment: DPoP requirement: when the operation is Tier 2 or Tier 3, when any requested Tier 1 scope has requires_dpop: true in the synced AIP Scope Catalog, or when endpoint policy requires DPoP, a DPoP proof JWT MUST be present in the DPoP HTTP header and the token MUST be presented with the DPoP authorization scheme. DPoP proofs MUST use EdDSA. The DPoP ath claim MUST equal BASE64URL(SHA-256(token)) over the exact compact token value from the Authorization header, excluding the scheme and whitespace. DPoP proof jti replay cache is separate from Credential Token jti cache and is keyed by (kid, jti).
